> Cursor-side mirror of `/contribute`. Source of truth: `.claude/skills/contribute/SKILL.md`.


# /contribute

Shepherd a new teammate (or a returning one on a fresh machine) through the minimum setup needed to make a valid first PR.

## When to run

- First-time onboarding on a fresh clone
- After a major tooling change (mise, pnpm major bump) to re-verify environment
- When switching between Cursor and Claude Code to ensure both IDEs are rule-synced

## Procedure

### 1. Toolchain check

Run and report:

```bash
mise --version          # expect ≥ 2024.x
node --version          # expect matching mise.toml
pnpm --version          # expect matching mise.toml
git --version           # expect ≥ 2.40
```

If any tool is missing, tell the user exactly what to install. Do not auto-install.

### 2. Repo state check

```bash
git status --short
git branch --show-current    # expect "v3" during the rebuild
git remote -v
```

If on `main` during the rebuild, prompt them to switch to `v3`.

### 3. Per-user config templates

- Check `.claude/settings.local.json` — if absent, prompt to copy from `.claude/settings.local.example.json` and edit
- Check `.claude/config/reserved-identifiers.json` — if absent, prompt to copy from `.claude/config/reserved-identifiers.example.json` and populate (per ADR #0004)
- Check `.env.local` files under `apps/*` (once Phase 2 has scaffolded them) — prompt to copy from `.env.example` if absent. Remind: credentials (OANDA, Anthropic, webhook tokens) go in the in-app Settings UI, not in `.env.local` (rule 11)

### 4. IDE check

- If working in Cursor, verify `.cursor/rules/` is present and up to date (run `scripts/sync-ide-rules.mjs` once Sub-phase 7 has shipped it)
- If working in Claude Code, verify the project path is in the user's additional-working-directories config (reduces per-write prompts)

### 5. Rule orientation

Walk the user through these reads, in order, with a one-line what-it-is for each:

- `.claude/README.md` — agent config catalog
- `.claude/CLAUDE.md` — the non-negotiables
- `.claude/context/domain.md` — what ForexFlow is
- `.claude/context/stack.md` — target stack
- `.claude/context/conventions.md` — naming, imports, commits
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` — PR workflow
- `.claude/plans/active.md` — current phase

### 6. First-PR checklist

Produce a checklist tailored to what the user said they want to work on:

- [ ] Branch off `v3` named `<type>/<scope>/<short-description>`
- [ ] Read the rules whose `scope` matches the files you'll touch
- [ ] If scaffolding a feature: ensure a requirement exists (mint with `requirements-curator` if not)
- [ ] Write implementation and tests in the same commit (TDD-concurrent per rule 02)
- [ ] Use `/review` and `/security-review` before committing
- [ ] Conventional commit message with scope from the enum
- [ ] PR template filled out completely
- [ ] `/verify` passes locally

### 7. Pointers for common first tasks

Match the user's stated intent to a skill:

- "Add a component" → `/add-component`
- "Add an API route" → `/add-api-route`
- "Fix a bug" → `/debug <symptom>` then implement
- "Write a requirement first" → `/trace --mint`
- "Just explore" → read `.claude/context/*`, then `git log --oneline` on a familiar area

## Output shape

```markdown
# Welcome to ForexFlow

## Environment

| Tool | Required | Installed | Status |
| ---- | -------- | --------- | ------ |
| mise | ≥ 2024   | 2026.1.2  | ✓      |
| node | 22.x     | 22.4.0    | ✓      |
| pnpm | 10.x     | 10.11.0   | ✓      |
| git  | ≥ 2.40   | 2.45.1    | ✓      |

## Repo

- Branch: `<current>`
- Working tree: `<status>`
- Remote: `<origin>`

## Per-user config

- [ ] `.claude/settings.local.json` — <created / exists / needs edit>
- [ ] `.claude/config/reserved-identifiers.json` — <created / exists>
- [ ] `.env.local` files — <status; "N/A until Phase 2" during the rebuild>

## IDE

- Cursor rules synced: yes / no / N/A
- Claude Code working directory allowed: yes / no / N/A

## Reading list (ordered)

1. `.claude/README.md`
2. `.claude/CLAUDE.md`
3. `.claude/context/domain.md`, `stack.md`, `conventions.md`
4. `CONTRIBUTING.md`
5. `.claude/plans/active.md`

## Your first PR checklist

- [ ] ...
- [ ] ...

## Next step

<tailored skill recommendation>
```

## What this skill does NOT do

- Install tools (the user runs installers)
- Commit any config on the user's behalf
- Read the user's home directory outside what's explicitly shown above
- Register personal names / emails anywhere (rule 00 §5)

## Exit

Prints the welcome/status report. Exits cleanly. Leaves the user ready to run their first skill or open their editor.
